architecture.summary: CHAMP is a benchmark dataset consisting of high school math competition problems annotated with concepts (general math facts) and hints (problem-specific tricks). The evaluation framework tests whether LLMs can leverage these annotations to improve reasoning. The system measures end-to-end answer correctness, effect of side information (hints/concepts/misleading facts), solution verification capability, and intermediate reasoning step quality. No novel model architecture is introduced; the work is a dataset and evaluation protocol for existing LLMs.

biblio.title: CHAMP: A Competition-level Dataset for Fine-Grained Analyses of LLMs’ Mathematical Reasoning Capabilities

claims.statement: The best model only scoring 58.1% in standard settings
With concepts and hints, performance sometimes improves, indicating that some models can make use of such side information
Models often arrive at the correct final answer through wrong reasoning steps
Most models struggle to verify solutions
Current evaluations mainly focus on the end-to-end final answer correctness, and it is unclear whether LLMs can make use of helpful side information such as problem-specific hints
